question_structure.context: Two drones, A and B, are conducting flight performance tests, flying along the same straight line in the same direction. At time $t=0$, the velocity of A is $v_{1}=16\mathbf{m}/\mathbf{s}$, and it is undergoing a uniformly decelerated linear motion with an acceleration of magnitude $a_{1}=2\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$ for an "in-air stop" test (i.e., decelerating until it stops in the air). At this moment, B is located 18 meters behind A, with a velocity of $v_{2}=4\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$ (in the same direction as $v_{1}$), and is undergoing a uniformly accelerated linear motion with an acceleration of magnitude $a_{2}=6\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$. To avoid collision with A ahead, at $t=3\mathrm{s}$, B begins to undergo a uniformly decelerated linear motion with an acceleration of magnitude $a_{0}$ for an "in-air stop".
question_structure.sub_question_1: Find the magnitude of the displacement of A in the first 3 seconds, $x_{1}$
question_structure.sub_question_2: Find the maximum distance between A and B within the first 3 seconds.
question_structure.sub_question_3: To avoid collision, the condition for the acceleration $a_{0}$ when B starts to decelerate must satisfy:

sub_question_1.step_1: From the problem statement, we have $x_{1}=v_{1}t-{\frac{1}{2}}{a_{1}}{t^{2}}=16{\times}3\mathrm{m}-{\frac{1}{2}}{\times}2{\times}3^{2}\mathrm{m}=39\mathrm{m}$.

sub_question_2.step_2: From the problem statement, it is known that when the two reach the same speed, $v_{1}-a_{1}t_{1}=v_{2}+a_{2}t_{1}$. Solving this gives $t_{1}=1.5\mathrm{s}$.
sub_question_2.step_3: At this moment, the speed of AB is $v_{a}=v_{1}-a_{1}t_{1}=13\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}=v_{B}$.
sub_question_2.step_4: During this period, the displacements of AB are respectively $x_{A}={\frac{v_{1}+v_{a}}{2}}t_{1}=21.75\mathrm{m}$, $x_{B}={\frac{v_{2}+v_{B}}{2}}t_{1}=12.75\mathrm{m}$.
sub_question_2.step_5: During this period, the displacement difference between the two is $\Delta x=x_{A}-x_{B}=9\mathrm{m}$.
sub_question_2.step_6: Therefore, the maximum distance between A and B at this moment is $\Delta x_{m}=18+\Delta x=27\mathrm{m}$.

sub_question_3.step_7: At 3 seconds, according to the problem, the velocities of AB are $v_{A1}=v_{1}-a_{1}t=10\mathrm{m/s}$, $v_{B1}=v_{2}+a_{2}t=22\mathrm{m/s}$
sub_question_3.step_8: From $x={\frac{(v_{0}+v)}{2}}t$, the displacement of AB during this period can be determined as $x_{A}=x_{B}=39\mathrm{m}$.
sub_question_3.step_9: The time required for $A$ to come to a stop is $t_{A}={\frac{v_{A1}}{a_{1}}}=5\mathrm{s}$.
sub_question_3.step_10: Therefore, after 3 seconds, the distance between A and B remains $s_{0}=18\mathrm{m}$.
sub_question_3.step_11: If A and B stop simultaneously and meet exactly, the relative displacement during this period is $\Delta x_{0}=\frac{v_{B1}}{2}\times t_{A}-\frac{v_{A1}}{2}\times t_{A}=30\mathbf{m}>s_{0}$, hence this scenario does not satisfy the condition.
sub_question_3.step_12: When B stops first and A stops later, the distance between A and B is minimized when they reach the same speed. To avoid collision, it is necessary to ensure that the relative displacement at the time of reaching the same speed is less than $s_{0} = 18\mathrm{m}$.
sub_question_3.step_13:
Let the time when the velocities become equal be $t_{0}$, and the magnitude of the equal velocity be $v_{all}$. We have
[
10 - 2t_{0} = 22 - a_{0}t_{0},
]
solving for $t_{0}$ gives
[
t_{0} = \frac{12}{a_{0} - 2},
]
and
[
v_{all} = 10 - \frac{24}{a_{0} - 2}.
]
sub_question_3.step_15: From the problem statement, the distance between the two satisfies $\Delta x=x_{A}+s_{0}-x_{B}\geq0$. Solving this, we obtain $a_{0}\geq\frac{72}{s_{0}}+2$. Substituting $s_{0}=18\mathrm{m}$ yields $a_{0}\geq6\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.
